candidates.best_signal: local M2 Studio hash-identical all-bucket save: +4.80% 3s, +5.36% 7s, +6.92% 10s, +7.24% 15s, +14.25% 30s versus same-binary serial shipped path
candidates.decision: keep enabled by default; fallback env KOKORO_DISABLE_DECODER_HNSF_OVERLAP=1
candidates.next_gate: quiet lower-end A/B; if a host regresses, use fallback and inspect decoder-pre CPU contention
candidates.quality: strict; overlap-only WAV hashes identical on all five buckets
candidates.scope: Swift runtime scheduling inside current Config F boundary

candidates.decision: keep; promote to quiet Irvine timing before replacing checked-in packages
candidates.next_gate: quiet Irvine M1 warmed run; quiet M2 Air rerun for fresh lower-end rows
candidates.quality: strict-like spotchecks: corr >=0.999994895, SNR >=46.51 dB, max abs <=0.008453

candidates.best_signal: initial local 3s CT9 +2.14%, but 10s -0.16% and 15s -0.27%; later same-process rows tied
candidates.decision: reject; toolchain metadata alone is below material threshold
candidates.next_gate: only revisit if paired with a real graph rewrite and same-process baseline
candidates.scope: single-package GeneratorFromHar rebuild with newer conversion target

candidates.decision: reject; split boundary/sync overhead exceeds body savings
candidates.next_gate: do not repeat broad exact split; only try if a Core ML call boundary is removed

candidates.best_signal: body-only is faster if x_source tensors are free (M2 17.6 vs 26.4 ms; Irvine 105.9 vs 168.3 ms), but full strict split loses once noise/source is included; decoderPre overlap cannot hide noise because noise waits for HAR and HnSF already exceeds decoderPre on measured 3s hosts; padded/Nyquist 3s source-noise split is quality-good but slower (34.4 vs 30.4 ms, -13.0%)
candidates.decision: reject; x_source body package is promising only with a cheaper strict source contract
candidates.next_gate: only revisit if x_source is produced without a separate Core ML noise call or with a new source representation
candidates.quality: strict on CPU+GPU; CPU+NE quality fails

candidates.best_signal: local E2E only wins 3s (49.532 ms vs production rewrite 49.669 ms) and noise-ties 10s
candidates.decision: reject as production replacement; simpler rewrite wins more buckets
candidates.next_gate: none unless a new operator rewrite changes runtime behavior beyond surface matching
candidates.quality: strict-like spotchecks: corr >=0.999993675, SNR >=48.01 dB, max abs <=0.007263

candidates.best_signal: local M2 Studio CPU+GPU generator-only: 3s -0.077 ms, 7s +0.414 ms versus plain prediction(from:)
candidates.decision: reject as current production win; keep --generator-output-backing as a device-check harness flag
candidates.next_gate: only revisit if a lower-end Mac or iPhone shows a material >1 ms warmed median gain
candidates.quality: strict; 3s waveform_full and trimmed waveform dumps were bit-identical, max_abs 0.0

candidates.best_signal: Irvine 3s +0.43%; M2 Studio strict point is slower (-1.15%)
candidates.decision: reject; less than one millisecond on M1 cannot close laishere gap
candidates.next_gate: do not repeat tail trim unless a new source/HAR representation removes much more padding
candidates.quality: strict at har_time=28561; shorter har_time=27601 fails max-abs gate
candidates.scope: single-package GeneratorFromHar with shorter strict HAR axis

candidates.best_signal: natural har_source is speed-positive but quality-failing; strict padded/Nyquist fused path has replacement-quality versus the current generator but no net win after Swift STFT credit (+0.051 ms 3s, +1.326 ms 7s, +2.231 ms 10s, +14.977 ms 30s); atan_swift fp32 raw phase is worse (-0.89 dB)
candidates.decision: reject; preserving strict source contract loses the speed edge
candidates.next_gate: new representation only: phase reparameterization, weight folding, or a no-extra-boundary Nyquist side input
candidates.quality: strict with padded geometry and dumped Nyquist phase

candidates.best_signal: local 3s -2.78% versus production upsample rewrite; CPU+NE still CPU-preferred after ANE compile failure
candidates.decision: reject; reproduces laishere-like LUT surface but is slower and does not fix placement
candidates.family: LUT-palettized full surface plus upsample rewrite
candidates.next_gate: do not repeat palettized final-waveform packages unless compression is moved behind a separate strict tail or changes placement
candidates.quality: strict by fused-output gate but weak margin: corr 0.999880, SNR 36.57 dB, max abs 0.009857
candidates.scope: single-package GeneratorFromHar with native-IN, broadcast AdaIN, fp16 inputs, pal8 weights, and zero-insert upsample rewrite

candidates.decision: reject as too small; graph-surface parity alone is not enough
candidates.family: Native-IN/broadcast/cos/fp16 fused surface without upsample rewrite
candidates.next_gate: do not repeat without a new layout, fusion, or partitioning mechanism

candidates.best_signal: padded buckets with oracle-fitted affine Nyquist repair reach only 26.46/27.69/27.64/27.04/26.36 dB SNR versus 50.06/49.14/49.87/49.21/48.42 dB with dumped Nyquist
candidates.decision: reject; a scalar or affine Nyquist calibration cannot replace the branch-sensitive Nyquist phase contract
candidates.next_gate: skip scalar Nyquist calibration; use branch-side input, phase reparameterization, weight folding, or learned adapter repair
candidates.quality: not strict; affine/negated scalar repairs fail the 45 dB padded waveform gate across all buckets

candidates.best_signal: Irvine 3s -3.0 ms; M2 Air 3s -2.2 ms; local native-IN variant only +0.07 ms
candidates.decision: reject; freezing style is not a speed path
candidates.next_gate: none unless combined with a material new operator rewrite
candidates.quality: strict on CPU+GPU; CPU+NE fails quality and is far slower
candidates.scope: single-package GeneratorFromHar with fixed af_heart projections

candidates.decision: not paper-strict; only useful with source recovery or no-ASR listening acceptance

next_actions: Run scripts/external_bakeoff/check_remote_host_quiet.py before any lower-end Mac promotion run.
Retest the HAR-post upsample rewrite on Irvine M1 and M2 Air only when outputs/external_bakeoff/remote_host_quiet_latest.md reports quiet=yes.
Do not use cold compile/cache timings; every frontier update must use warmed medians.
Use README/Notes/fixed-cost-latency-fit.md to separate fixed-boundary overhead from duration-scaled generator cost before promoting a new optimization family.

Use README/Notes/har-stft-phase-contract.md and scripts/external_bakeoff/summarize_hnsf_source_boundary.py before revisiting any har_source boundary; Swift STFT credit alone does not make the strict padded/Nyquist path win.
Use README/Kokoro-M1-HAR-STFT-contract-deep-research-prompt.md for the next external research pass; the source equation is solved, the HAR/STFT contract is not.
For a new strict candidate, require a single-package graph or a removed Core ML call boundary before lower-end promotion.
Run the installed Config F iPhone runner only after the physical iPhone is unlocked; current launch blocker is device_locked.
Keep fast F0/source branches separate from strict paper claims unless no-ASR human listening accepts the exact WAVs.
